title: Arena Elimination UI & Winner Selection
goal: Build the Streamlit UI for the arena elimination workflow: winner-selection controls per model response, Proceed to Next Round button, eliminated models panel, final winner banner, and Reset Arena button. Wire UI actions to the arena state machine from Part 02.

in_scope: Render 'Select as Winner' checkbox for each model result after responses are displayed
Render 'Proceed to Next Round' button that advances the arena when at least one winner is selected
Display blocking validation message when user attempts to proceed with zero winners
Show round badge/indicator near results header (e.g., 'Round 2 of Arena')
Display active model count alongside round indicator
Render eliminated models in a compact 'Eliminated' panel
Render final winner banner when arena completes (one model remains)
Render 'Reset Arena' button visible during active arena flow
Gate all arena UI behind feature_arena_elimination flag
Integrate arena init on first Submit when elimination is enabled
out_of_scope: Persistence or history of arena rounds (Week 3)

inputs: src/arena.py — arena state functions from Part 02
src/app.py — existing results rendering and session state
PRD Week 1 section 4 — Arena elimination flow
outputs: src/app.py — updated with arena elimination UI controls and flow integration
checklist: Import arena module functions in app.py
On Submit click with arena enabled, call init_arena() with selected deployments if arena not yet initialized
Modify inference call to use get_active_models() instead of full selected deployments when arena is active
After results rendering, display round indicator: 'Round {n}' with active model count
Render st.checkbox('Select as Winner') for each successful model result
Render 'Proceed to Next Round' button below winner checkboxes
On Proceed click, collect checked winner names and call advance_round(winner_names)
If zero winners selected on Proceed click, display st.error('Select at least one winner before proceeding')
After advance_round(), trigger inference for new active models automatically or prompt user
Render 'Eliminated' expander showing list of eliminated deployment names
When is_arena_completed() returns True, display st.success() winner banner with deployment name
Render 'Reset Arena' button in sidebar or main area; on click call reset_arena()
When feature_arena_elimination is False, skip all arena UI and use existing comparison flow
acceptance_criteria: Each model result shows a 'Select as Winner' control after responses are rendered
Proceed action is blocked if zero winners are selected with a clear error message
Unselected models move to eliminated list and are excluded from next round inference
Round number increments by 1 when proceeding
Final winner banner appears when one active model remains
Eliminated models are listed in a compact panel
Reset control clears arena state and returns to initial pre-round state
Arena controls are hidden when feature_arena_elimination is false
Arena state survives Streamlit reruns within a session
review_focus: Checkbox state management across Streamlit reruns (use session state keys for checkboxes)
Winner selection validation happens before state mutation
Reset function fully clears state without leaving orphan keys
Arena flow integrates cleanly with existing comparison and export sections
UI layout remains clean and usable with 2-5 active models
risks_or_notes: Streamlit reruns on every interaction — checkbox state drift between rounds is a known challenge
Consider using unique keys for checkboxes per round to prevent stale state
The Proceed button may need a form boundary to prevent premature reruns
If user changes sidebar deployment selection during active arena, decide whether to invalidate the arena
Long-running arenas with many rounds may accumulate significant session state
definition_of_done: Arena elimination UI renders correctly when flag is enabled
Full flow works: select models → submit → pick winners → proceed → repeat → final winner
Arena is invisible when flag is disabled
Round transitions execute without app crash for 2-5 models
UI is clean and responsive across elimination rounds
